question_text: Match the following terms to the correct example:

answers.text: Obtain an approximate value for an unknown quantity.
Give the precise meaning of a word, phrase, concept or physical quantity.
Display information in a diagrammatic or logical form.

Credit sources of information used by referencing (or citing), following one recognized referencing system. References should be included in the text and also at the end of the piece of work in a reference list or bibliography.
Break down in order to bring out the essential elements or structure. (To identify parts and relationships, and to interpret information to reach conclusions.)
Produce a plan, simulation or model.
Make an appraisal by weighing up the strengths and limitations.
Give a detailed account including reasons and causes. (See also “Justify”.)
Obtain an answer showing relevant stages in the working.
Give a detailed account or picture of a situation, event, pattern or process.
Offer a considered and balanced review that includes a range of arguments, factors or hypotheses. Opinions or conclusions should be presented clearly and supported by appropriate evidence.
Give a judgment based on a given statement or result of a calculation.
Obtain a numerical answer showing the relevant stages in the working.
Arrange or order by class or category.
Express precisely and systematically the relevant concept(s) or argument(s).
Use knowledge and understanding in response to a given situation or real circumstances. Use an idea, equation, principle, theory or law in relation to a given problem or issue.

question_text: Please write your English name used in class:

G6A = Grade 6 Class A
G6B = Grade 6 Class B
G6C = Grade 6 Class C
